figures.title: Quantifying Sub-Optimality in Routing for Automated Market Makers
figures.caption: Fig. 3. Effect of delayed state on performance (relative to FVO and G-FVO). The gap between N = 0 and N = 1 quantifies the cost of one-block staleness (oracle vs. implementable planning), while the monotone drift as N increases captures the growing loss from routing on progressively older snapshots. — This figure compares the performance of FVO (Fresh Value Oracle) and G-FVO (Gas-aware FVO) routing strategies across planning horizons with varying state staleness. figures.title: Quantum Kernels and the Cross-Section of Stock Returns: Anatomy of a Vanishing Advantage
